title: Park silent approvals without deciding them
description: Replace indefinite in-memory approval resource retention with a durable pause/resume lifecycle. After an operational threshold, release workers, API slots, and transport resources while preserving the approval as unresolved durable state. Resume must reconstruct the pending decision and evidence transparently. Silence, parking, restart, and reclamation must never grant or deny authority.
close_reason: Implemented durable no-decision approval parking with bounded resource cleanup, restart reconciliation, verification-first resume, operator surfaces, concurrency/race/fuzz/PostgreSQL coverage, and documentation.

title: Publish Thornhill under AGPL-3.0
description: Add the canonical GNU Affero General Public License v3.0, align repository and container metadata, obtain exact-SHA GitHub Actions evidence, make the sanitized replacement repository public, then apply and verify branch protection and public-repository security controls.
close_reason: AGPL-3.0 publication, exact-SHA CI, public visibility, branch protection, secret scanning, push protection, private vulnerability reporting, and CodeQL default setup were applied and verified.

title: Harden containers and add maintained security analysis
description: Research current Docker, Google, Chainguard, GitHub, and supply-chain guidance. Harden container build/runtime; add Dependabot-covered static analysis, vulnerability/misconfiguration/image scans, SBOM evidence, policy tests, and documentation; validate through protected CI.
close_reason: Closed remaining PostgreSQL runtime gap: read-only root, bounded tmpfs, least init capabilities, UID 70 assertion, revision-tagged CI-gated DB image deployment/rollback, and live runtime verification.

title: Clean supply-chain noise and add provenance controls
description: Remove broken/misleading Dependabot approval automation, make periodic deploy deferrals quiet without weakening safety, verify immutable dependency pins, add OpenSSF Scorecard coverage, and document/measurably improve SLSA/OpenSSF posture.

title: Fence job transitions and make queue delivery durable
description:
Context: Language review found queued cancellation can still execute, answer delivery can overwrite terminal state, job/queue insertion has a crash window, and deployment drain guards inserts but not resume updates.
Scope: internal/dispatch, internal/store, internal/bridge admission, deployment transition guard.
Acceptance criteria:
- Cancelled/terminal jobs never start Hermes work.
- Answer cannot overwrite terminal state.
- Queued jobs are transactionally delivered or deterministically reconciled after crash.
- Deployment pause blocks every transition into runnable/active states.
- Focused race/integration regressions pass.
Risk notes: preserve approval authority and exact-once/non-idempotent boundaries.
close_reason: Durable transition/queue coordination, exact-run admission fencing, cancellation races, deployment guard, and PostgreSQL regressions implemented; full local Go race and integration gates pass.

title: Reclaim blocked workers and own backend lifecycles
description:
Context: needs_input/silent streams can exhaust River workers; event persistence blocks/reorders publishers; approval lock is global; Desk shutdown is not application-owned.
Scope: internal/bridge, internal/events, internal/gateway, cmd/thornhill, focused tests.
Acceptance criteria:
- Non-authority waits release scarce River workers through durable continuation semantics.
- Stalled stream phases are bounded without imposing a whole-job deadline.
- Event delivery order is deterministic and producer latency is bounded.
- Shutdown cancels/waits Desk before River/store close.
- Approval serialization remains fail-closed while unrelated jobs do not block each other.
close_reason: Durable input parking, stream-idle recovery, ordered bounded event persistence, per-run approval locks, and owned Desk shutdown implemented; race tests pass.

title: Add frontend protocol and lifecycle harness
description:
Context: unchecked frames can crash rendering; half-open WebSockets do not reconnect; async media ownership, retention, accessibility, and testability are incomplete.
Scope: web/src, web package/tooling, Go/frontend event contract test.
Acceptance criteria:
- Runtime guards reject malformed protocol frames.
- Every Go event kind is handled or intentionally ignored by an executable contract.
- Heartbeat expiry reconnects once and stop prevents future reconnects.
- Media/ICE/fetch ownership is cancellable and tested.
- Active jobs remain while terminal history is bounded.
- Link/error state is semantically announced.
- Frontend tests run in CI.
close_reason: Runtime protocol guards, event contract test, heartbeat reconnect ownership, media generation cancellation, bounded board, accessibility landmarks, and CI-enforced Vitest harness validated

title: Enforce operational policy and repair runbook drift
description:
Context: hardening harness duplicates Compose policy; CHECK_ONLY lock contention can report success; Dependabot review omits commit_id; rollback compatibility is untested; docs have deployment/link drift.
Scope: scripts, workflows, Compose/config, README/docs.
Acceptance criteria:
- Hardening checks assert the actual Compose model including app PID limit.
- CHECK_ONLY never succeeds without checking correspondence.
- Dependabot review binds the CI-tested SHA and CI policy enforces it.
- Migration/rollback compatibility policy is executable or explicitly gated.
- Runbook accurately describes db+app recreation and vendored link typo is fixed.
- Existing assurance checks remain intact.
close_reason: Compose-derived hardening, fail-closed CHECK_ONLY, commit-bound Dependabot approval, schema rollback gate, and runbook corrections implemented; policy, Actionlint, ShellCheck, and Compose checks pass.

title: Integrate and ship language-review remediation
description:
Context: Final review, CI, deployment, and live verification after all remediation waves.
Acceptance criteria:
- Focused regressions and all repository quality/security gates pass.
- Review findings are documented with resolved/deferred rationale.
- PR checks are green on exact SHA.
- CI-gated deployment and local/tailnet revision checks pass.
- Agent Mail reservations released.
